prediction.reasoning: Predicting stock prices is inherently uncertain due to the volatile nature of the market and its reaction to new information. These confidence intervals are hypothetical and not based on a specific model. They assume a normal market behavior and take into account typical short-term volatility, providing a range in which the price might fall within based on historical price changes over similar periods.
